title: Vijay holds key meeting as TVK eyes government formation in Tamil Nadu
content: Effectively, the party has 107 MLAs as TVK president C. Joseph Vijay has won from both Perambur in Chennai and Tiruchi East.

title: POLYCET 2026: 91.37% pass in Andhra Pradesh; girls outdo boys, ASR district tops list
content: A total of 1,48,950 candidates qualify for diploma admissions; girls record 93.57% pass rate against 89.88% for boys; over 84,000 seats on offer across 265 polytechnic institutions for 2026-27

title: Assam poll verdict rejection of blue blood politics: Himanta Biswa Sarma
content: The Chief Minister says that the sons of the State’s three former Chief Ministers are yet to create an identity independent of their fathers

title: TVK changes Tamil Nadu’s political landscape | Data
content: Actor C. Joseph Vijay’s Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam has, in its electoral debut, decimated the DMK’s seat base in the urban heartlands where the ruling party had been strongest. The AIADMK held its ground in the west and north through coalition cohesion, and the DMK clung on best in its delta heartland.

title: Assembly election 2026 results LIVE: M.K. Stalin steps down as Tamil Nadu CM; 'People’s will is supreme, we respect the mandate,’ says TMC's Mahua Moitra
content: A communication to this effect has been sent to Tamil Nadu Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar, who is expected to request M.K. Stalin to continue as interim Chief Minister, until the new government assumes office

title: Three from ‘Lottery King’ Santiago Martin’s family win for different parties in Tamil Nadu, Puducherry
content: Santiago Martin, often referred to as ‘Lottery King’ and founder of Martin Group, now has three MLA-elects in his family

title: DMK’s outgoing MLA Paranthaman demands action against Sekarbabu for Stalin’s defeat in Kolathur
content: In a social media message, I. Paranthaman asked, “How could a person responsible for the defeat of our leader stand before him without any shame, fear, or embarrassment?”

title: Telangana’s summers impact women harder in rural and low-income settings; nutrition services take a hit
content: Prolonged exposure can lead to dehydration, heat exhaustion and more serious conditions, particularly in settings where access to timely medical care may be limited

title: Bihar Govt's Cabinet expansion on May 7; PM Modi, Amit Shah to attend: State BJP chief
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Home Minister Amit Shah, BJP national president Nitin Nabin, and other senior leaders will be present on the Cabinet expansion, Bihar BJP chief Sanjay Saraogi said

title: Punjab CM Mann meets President Murmu; demands cancelling membership of six Rajya Sabha MPs from State
content: On April 24, the Aam Aadmi Party suffered a jolt when seven of its 10 Rajya Sabha MPs quit and merged with the BJP

title: ​Lost and found: On the 2026 Assembly elections
content: The National Democractic Alliance’s success in Bengal and Assam is partly offset by its failure in Tamil Nadu and Kerala

title: Raghav Chadha meets President Murmu, alleges harassment of MPs who quit AAP and joined BJP
content: Raghav Chadha alleged that several former AAP MPs who have joined the BJP are facing intimidation and coercive actions from State agencies.

title: Rare caracals spotted in Thar Desert near India-Pakistan border
content: The feline’s population has declined by more than 95% due to habitat loss, fragmentation, and retaliatory killings

title: PM Modi condemns attack on Indians in Fujairah Petroleum Zone
content: On May 4, 2026, three Indian nationals were injured after a fire broke out on the Fujairah Petroleum Industries Zone (FOIZ) in the UAE following a drone strike originating from Iran

title: Put petty politics aside; it's not about one party, but India: Rahul on TMC's loss in polls
content: Mr. Gandhi also came out in support of the TMC, which has been trounced by the BJP in the polls, and urged those gloating over the loss of Mamata Banerjee's party to put petty politics aside

title: DMK’s vote share of 1.54 crore shows people’s faith, says M.K. Stalin
content: Mr. Stalin said it was the duty of the DMK to meet voters and thank them personally, and he called upon party candidates to do so

title: Two Indian journalists win Pulitzer Prize for highlighting cyber fraud
content: Anand RK and Suparna Sharma won the award in the Illustrated Reporting and Commentary category for their work highlighting digital surveillance and cyber fraud

title: BJP State chief P.V.N. Madhav asks party cadre to stay among public, work for welfare
content: State BJP president addresses victory celebrations at Srikalahasti over party’s performance in West Bengal and Assam; says party will not tolerate sacrilege in temples, refers to Tirumala ghee adulteration issue

title: Congress slams Govt over ‘sluggishness’ in private investment, flags ED-CBI-IT ‘raid raj’
content: Congress general secretary in-charge communications Jairam Ramesh said that for some time now, his party has been drawing attention to a fundamental problem preventing the economy from achieving higher real GDP growth rates

title: Will of the people is supreme, says Mahua Moitra after BJP's victory in West Bengal
content: "We fought the good fight against unimaginable odds on an uneven pitch, and for that I am proud of my leader and my party," says Ms. Moitra

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: UDF win was made massive by anti-Left voting
content: Senior leaders V.D. Satheesan,  Ramesh Chennithala, and K.C. Venugopal attribute the massive win to cohesive teamwork within the alliance, the guarantees and assurances given to the people, methodical electioneering, and the ‘misgovernance and nepotism’ of the outgoing LDF government.  The scale of the Left’s defeat has been amplified by the loss of face in some of its strongholds such as Taliparamba, Payyannur, and Ambalapuzha, where CPI(M) rebels backed by the UDF posted decisive victories

title: Karnataka bypolls results 2026: Congress retains Bagalkot and Davangere South Assembly constituencies
content: Umesh Meti won Bagalkot seat by a margin of 22,332 votes, while Samarth Mallikarjun won Davangere South seat by a difference of 5,708 votes, the Election Commission said.

title: DMK’s fortress of Chennai breached like never before
content: The DMK’s downfall in Chennai is drastic compared to the previous election in 2021, in which the alliance it led not only won all the 16 constituencies in the district, but also secured over 50% of vote share in 11 of them

title: In northern districts, the Dravidian majors fight the TVK wave
content: Ministers Duraimurugan, Gandhi, deputy speaker Pitchandi lose; E.V.Velu, MRK Panneerselvam, Premallatha win

title: In Assam, BJP wave topples sons of three former CMs
content: This election not only solidified the BJP’s dominance but also saw the defeat of the sons of three former Chief Ministers—Gaurav Gogoi, Debabrata Saikia, and Diganta Barman

title: Adoption requests pour in for abandoned Siberian huskies; three more dogs rescued on Shankarpally–Patancheru road
content: Blood tests show some parameters are irregular, likely due to dehydration. Police register Cruelty case. Search for more dogs continue.

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: CPI suffers major setback, seat count halved
content: The outcome marks a sharp decline from the performance in the Assembly polls of 2021, when the party had won 17 seats.  Despite the overall decline, three of party’s incumbent Ministers managed to emerge victorious

title: Space Wrap: From Sriharikota to Leh, preparations for Gaganyaan mission in full swing
content: This article is part of Space Wrap, a round-up of space news and developments in and related to India every month

title: Assam, Bengal poll outcomes indicate minorities alone cannot help Congress retain power in Karnataka in 2028
content: Analysts note that broader voter consolidation, including among majority communities, can play a crucial role in determining outcomes in Assembly elections

title: Festival showcasing pastoral cultures to begin from May 8
content: The four-day festival organised by the Centre for Pastoralism and the Pune International Centre will also have an economic consultation on ‘Revival of Desi Oon’ to secure pastoral livelihoods in the Deccan region

title: DMK would function as efficient Opposition, says M.K. Stalin
content: In a social media message, the DMK president said he accepted the people’s verdict and congratulated the victors on their win

title: DMK retains core vote share with better seat conversion, but stalwarts lose
content: The party was eclipsed by TVK not only in Chennai — long considered its stronghold — but also in Tiruvallur, Chengalpattu, and Kancheepuram districts. Ministers who lost include Duraimurugan, T.M. Anbarasan, T.R.B. Rajaa, and Ma. Subramanian

title: KSRTC bus catches fire on road in Haveri district of Karnataka, completely gutted, passengers safe
content: The mishap occurred near Devaragudda in Ranebennur taluk when a ‘Pallakki’ bus of KSRTC’s was proceeding from Bengaluru towards Gadag

title: Strict action against anyone forcibly occupying others' land: Yogi Adityanath
content: Yogi Adityanath met around 200 people during the programme held in the morning and personally reached out to them as they sat in front of the Mahant Digvijaynath Smriti Bhavan premises

title: Police arrest Maoist leader from Jharkhand’s Latehar
content: The 42-year-old man has been associated with the TSPC for the past 15 years. At least 16 criminal cases are pending against him in multiple districts of the state, a senior police officer said.

title: AICC general secretary Venugopal signals Congress’ ‘openness’ to talks with TVK in Tamil Nadu
content: The Congress general secretary stated that a “new situation” had emerged in Tamil Nadu. “Congress would factor in the emerging scenario and decide accordingly”

title: BJP’s Bengal sweep fires up Punjab unit; AAP moves to deflate buzz
content: State BJP president says law and order situation in Punjab similar to West Bengal; CM cites crackdown on farmers agitation

title: Malayalam actor Santhosh Nair dies in road accident
content: Having acted in over a hundred films as well as television serials since the early 1980s, Santhosh was a familiar face to the Malayalam audience

title: What the winner must learn from the loser in Kerala: No leader is inevitable
content: The LDF’s campaign, like its government, was strikingly at odds with Left tradition and revolved around Pinarayi’s personality; the UDF must now resist the temptation to imitate this model

title: India at Venice Biennale 2026 curtain raiser: Many voices, one resonance
content: From soil and thread to immersive myth and monumental sculpture, India’s Venice Biennale 2026 presence expands beyond the pavilion into a layered meditation on home and identity

title: West Bengal election results: Full list of winners
content: The elections were held under the shadow of the SIR exercise, in which the names of around 91 lakh people were removed from the electoral rolls, which shrank to around 6.82 crore.

title: West Bengal Assembly results: TMC alleges post-poll violence as many party offices vandalised
content: BJP Leader Rahul Sinha, however, rejected the charge that any of his party's workers were involved in vandalism, saying it might be the handiwork of rival factions within the TMC

title: West Bengal election results updates: Result on final seat awaited, as BJP crosses majority mark
content: Not only has the BJP been able to hold its strongholds of north Bengal and southwest Bengal but made significant inroads in erstwhile Trinamool bastions of Kolkata and adjoining district.

title: Despite severe setbacks in Assam and Tamil Nadu, not all is lost for Congress
content: The party may have failed to win in Assam and Tamil Nadu, but in its defeat there is a silver lining: its pre-eminence in the INDIA bloc is further cemented

title: Das Adam Smith Problem: rethinking Smith’s moral and economic worlds
content: The Das Adam Smith Problem shows an apparent dichotomy between sympathy and self-interest in Adam Smith’s works, but most scholars now view it as a misunderstanding, arguing that his philosophy forms a coherent whole uniting ethics and economics

title: Stay indoors between noon and 4 p.m., Yadgir residents told
content: Deputy Commissioner and Chairman of District Disaster Management Authority Harshal Bhoyar has issued an alert notice to the public

content: Pixxel will design, build, launch, and operate the satellite, and Sarvam will provide the AI backbone

content: No human casualties have been reported. However, a dog domesticated within the warehouse died in the fire.

title: "Been In Power 6 Times, No Defeat We Have Not Faced": MK Stalin's 1st Reaction
content: MK Stalin, who took up the reins as chief minister in 2021, faced his first electoral defeat in 35 years, losing his Kolathur bastion to TVK's VS Babu.

content: The Treasury Secretary also criticised China, alleging Beijing was financially enabling Iran. "Iran is the largest state sponsor of terrorism, and China has been buying 90 per cent of their energy, so they are funding the largest state sponsor of terrorism," he said.

content: The composition of the Vijay Cabinet promises to be a radical departure from the status quo, favouring a technocratic and representative mix over old-school patronage.

content: Contesting on a BJP ticket, Maji ran a grassroots campaign, reaching voters door to door and secured over 1 lakh votes in the Ausgram constituency.

content: Pixxel plans to design, build, launch and operate the Pathfinder satellite, a 200 kilogram class spacecraft. Sarvam will provide the AI backbone.

content: Tamil Nadu Assembly Election Results 2026 LIVE Updates: While most exit polls have predicted DMK's second straight win, one suggested a huge wave of the "Vijay effect" in the state this time.

title: Opinion: Opinion | What Our 'Liberals' Really Need To Understand About Bengal's Voters
content: If liberal principles are to mean anything, they cannot be contingent on geography or convenience. The borders of Bengal cannot be where those principles end. Bengal's voters are not passive participants in someone else's narrative.

title: US To Pakistan, How International Media Covered BJP's Victory In Bengal
content: From London to New York and Islamabad to Dhaka, publications also gave prominent space to Tamil superstar Joseph Vijay's surprise victory in Tamil Nadu.

content: Commercial LPG Prices Hiked By Rs 993 Amid Iran-US War, No Changes In Domestic Rates, Petrol, Diesel Prices

content: Seoul said on Tuesday that authorities will investigate the cause of an explosion and fire on a South Koreanoperated ship in the Strait of Hormuz in an incident

title: After Trinamool Defeat, BJP's Suvendu Adhikari's "Akhilesh Next" Warning
content: Suvendu Adhikari likened the TMC to the Aam Aadmi Party, calling it corrupt, family-oriented and devoid of any ideology.

title: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: "Stop Holding World Hostage," Germany Tells Iran After UAE Attacked
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: The attacks shattered a period of relative calm in the Middle East since the ceasefire took effect on April 8.

title: Against Anyone Forcibly Occupying Others' Land: Yogi Adityanath
content: Yogi Adityanath assures prompt resolution of public grievances and strict action on illegal land grabbing in UP.

title: "Fascinated, Disturbed": US Judge 'Apologises' To Trump Event Shooting Suspect
content: A U.S. judge on Monday apologized to the man accused of attempting to assassinate President Donald Trump for the "legally deficient" treatment he has

title: 3 Family Members, 3 Parties, 3 Wins: Big Draw For Lottery Baron In Polls
content: Santiago Martin's wife, son, and son-in-law contested from three different parties and drew victories in their respective constituencies.

title: IPL: What’s gone wrong for LSG? Pant’s form, captaincy, batting woes derail campaign
content: Lucknow Super Giants face a dire IPL 2026 season, reeling from six consecutive losses and languishing at the bottom. Captain Rishabh Pant's form and leadership are under scrutiny, while batting collapses and inconsistent bowling plague the team. With playoff hopes fading, LSG desperately needs an extraordinary turnaround to salvage their campaign.

title: Mamata 'Bye'-nerjee: BJP tide leaves Bengal's big fish stranded
content: Bengal has witnessed a seismic political shift as the BJP surged to power, unseating Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress after 15 years. The saffron party secured a decisive victory, breaching the majority mark and fulfilling Amit Shah's ambitious target. This marks a significant change in the state's political landscape, ending decades of Left and TMC dominance.

title: GitHub COO on AI 'Shit Code' overload that many users say has been degrading coding platform
content: GitHub COO Kyle Daigle has confirmed the platform is processing 275 million commits per week—up from one billion across all of 2025—as AI agent workflows overwhelm infrastructure never designed for autonomous coding. Two major incidents in April broke thousands of repositories, pushing uptime below 85 percent. Ghostty developer Mitchell Hashimoto, GitHub's 1,299th user, has since announced he's leaving the platform entirely.

title: 'I am their next target': Raghav Chadha alleges 'vendetta politics' by Punjab govt
content: Rajya Sabha member Raghav Chadha accused the AAP-led Punjab government of using state machinery for political vendetta against MPs who joined the BJP. He alleged selective targeting through various agencies, presenting these concerns to the President. Another rebel MP, Sandeep Pathak, stated they sought constitutional safeguards from the President, who assured protection.

title: 'I was never rated as a T20 player': KL Rahul's candid admission
content: KL Rahul is enjoying a stellar IPL 2026 season, leading Delhi Capitals in runs with 433 in 9 matches. He reflects on overcoming the 'Test specialist' tag to become an all-format player, emphasizing his pride in adapting to T20 demands, particularly maximising the Powerplay for his team.

title: India among most resilient large EMs, better placed for future global shocks: Moody’s
content: In its latest study on emerging-market sovereigns, the agency notes that India has ranked among the more resilient economies since 2020, based on multiple indicators such as sovereign bond spreads, domestic yield movements, and exchange-rate stability.

title: JP Morgan CEO wants companies to get rid of some managers; here’s why
content: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon identified bureaucracy as a 'silent killer' that breeds complacency and inefficiency within organizations. He urged companies to eliminate managers who foster such practices, emphasizing the need to focus on outcomes and share information transparently. Dimon advocates for assigning critical tasks to small, focused teams to increase accountability and speed up decision-making.

title: Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw picks niece Claire Mazumdar as successor at Biocon: Report
content: Claire Mazumdar, 37 years of age, currently heads Bicara Therapeutics as its founder and CEO. The firm, which was incubated by Biocon and later listed on NASDAQ, went public in 2024 and now has a market valuation of over $1.6 billion.

title: Indian esports scales up, but still awaits its 1983 Cricket World Cup moment
content: India’s esports ecosystem is scaling rapidly, backed by global exposure and new regulation, but still lacks a defining breakthrough. Animesh Agarwal outlines the gaps - structure, perception, and global success - even as S8UL expands internationally. For India, the shift from potential to proof hinges on a global win that can transform esports into a mainstream sporting narrative.

title: 'Get out of your God complex...': What Jensen Huang said to Dario Amodei
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang dismisses AI job loss predictions as 'ridiculous,' urging tech leaders to abandon their 'God complex.' He argues AI is a job creator, citing increased demand for software engineers and the Jevons paradox, where efficiency drives greater consumption and thus more jobs. Huang warns that discouraging technical careers could harm the US economy.

title: 'No proposal before the govt': Centre rules out relief for state oil firms
content: State-run fuel retailers will not receive government financial backing for losses incurred from selling petrol, diesel, and aviation turbine fuel below cost. Despite rising crude oil prices, the government has opted to shield consumers by not revising retail prices for these fuels, focusing instead on price adjustments for bulk diesel and commercial LPG.

title: Indian firms go big in America: Pour in $16.4 bn, create over 70,000 jobs across US
content: Indian companies are significantly boosting the US economy with substantial investments, creating thousands of jobs, and driving innovation.  Senior officials highlighted nearly $16.4 billion in cumulative FDI, supporting over 70,000 jobs and significant R&D.  Further commitments are anticipated, underscoring India's growing role in key American sectors.

title: Commanders, Raiders Eye Former Cowboys CB Trevon Diggs in Free Agency Rumors
content: The Washington Commanders and the Las Vegas Raiders are exploring a move for Trevon Diggs as teams reassess needs after the NFL Draft. The former Dallas Cowboys All-Pro remains a free agent following an inconsistent 2025 season. With strong ties to Dan Quinn, Washington could offer the right system for a comeback. Meanwhile, the Raiders need depth in the secondary. Diggs’ ball-hawking ability still stands out despite injuries and recent struggles, making him a low-risk option with upside as training camp approaches

title: Viral Clip: IShowSpeed Lands Punch on Fan During Appearance in Puerto Rico
content: Popular streamer IShowSpeed's recent public appearance in Puerto Rico took a dramatic turn when a playful boxing-style moment with a fan went viral.  During a meet-up, Speed landed a strong punch, causing the fan to stumble, which quickly ignited online discussions and exaggerated claims of a knockout. This incident highlights the unpredictable nature of his content tours.

title: Candace Owens Claims Ben Shapiro Lawsuits; Laura Loomer Fires Back With Criticism
content: Candace Owens and Laura Loomer are in a heated public dispute, with Owens accusing Ben Shapiro of legal attacks and Loomer challenging her claims. The conflict includes allegations about lawsuits, private data access, and media influence. Other figures like Ana Kasparian are also involved. Many claims remain unverified, making the situation complex and still developing.

title: ‘95 off 30 balls!’: Langer cites practice blitz to back ‘selfless’ Pant despite LSG defeat
content: Despite another loss in IPL 2026, Lucknow Super Giants coach Justin Langer backed captain Rishabh Pant, highlighting a recent practice innings of 95 off 30 balls as proof he's close to his best. Langer defended Pant's decision to bat lower in the order, calling it a selfless move for the team's benefit.

title: Who will be West Bengal CM? Suvendu Adhikari, Dilip Ghosh top contenders
content: The BJP has secured a decisive victory in West Bengal, ending the Trinamool Congress's 15-year reign with a two-thirds majority. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee's defeat marks a significant political shift. The party is now poised to form the government, with the selection of the next chief minister being the immediate focus.

title: Bookie allegedly shot dead by Khalistani shooters in Canada; gang links under probe
content: A man, identified by the alias Sam Canada and known as a bookie, was fatally shot in a Surrey office building, believed to be a targeted execution. Authorities are investigating potential links to pro-Khalistan shooters with Indian connections. A fugitive gangster claimed responsibility, alleging the victim was involved in match-fixing and betting, warning of further actions.

title: Hardik Pandya update: Ryan Rickelton shares latest on MI skipper’s illness
content: Mumbai Indians secured a vital IPL 2026 victory, yet captain Hardik Pandya's absence due to back spasms casts a shadow. Opener Ryan Rickelton expressed uncertainty about Pandya's return, admitting he's unaware of the issue's severity. Suryakumar Yadav captained in Pandya's stead as MI chased down a massive target, keeping playoff hopes alive.

title: Gold price prediction: Where are prices headed today - Check outlook for today
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are expected to continue seeing volatility in the near-term, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: Left out: Kerala gone, Communists not in office in any state after almost 50 years
content: Kerala's voters have ousted the CPM-led LDF, marking the first time since 1977 India has no Communist-governed state. The Congress-led UDF's victory, fueled by anti-incumbency, signals a significant challenge for the Left's national relevance and may impact the INDIA bloc's dynamics.  Defeat was compounded by the win of CPM rebels.

title: A star is born in Tamil Nadu politics: Quiet, introverted, persistent
content: Vijay's transformation from a shy child star to a perseverant cinematic icon is not just a tale of talent, but a narrative of determination that now propels him towards a political career. His formative years, filled with challenges and triumphs exemplified by the hit 'Poove Unakkaga,' reveal a spirit that refuses to wane.

title: 'Do not tell me again ... not acceptable': Gavaskar blasts Bumrah over no-ball errors
content: Jasprit Bumrah is experiencing a difficult IPL 2026 season, having taken only three wickets in ten matches, contributing to Mumbai Indians' struggles. Despite a recent win, Bumrah's performance against Lucknow Super Giants, including multiple no-balls, drew criticism from Sunil Gavaskar. Rohit Sharma and Ryan Rickelton's partnership secured Mumbai's victory.

title: IPL 2026 playoff scenarios: LSG's hopes all but gone after six straight losses
content: Despite a blistering 63 off 21 balls from Nicholas Pooran and a strong partnership, Lucknow Super Giants suffered their sixth consecutive defeat. Their inability to close out games, even after posting a massive 228, has left them virtually out of the IPL 2026 playoff race. With five must-win games remaining, a miraculous recovery is needed. LSG needs a perfect run and other results to go their way to have a miraculous recovery this IPL 2026 season.

title: Record low: Rupee falls to 95.40 against US dollar
content: The Indian rupee plunged to a record low of 95.40 against the US dollar on Tuesday, extending its decline amid escalating Middle East tensions and elevated crude oil prices. This global uncertainty is impacting financial markets, with Indian benchmark indices also trading lower. Analysts anticipate continued pressure on the rupee due to persistent dollar demand and foreign fund outflows.

title: IPL 2026: Time for Sanju Samson to silence doubters
content: Sanju Samson returns to the Arun Jaitley Stadium, a venue where his international career once teetered. Now a CSK superstar, his high stock is a testament to his T20 World Cup turnaround.  He faces a crucial test against Delhi Capitals, needing to replicate his form to secure a spot in India's 2027 ODI World Cup plans.

title: 'Will poison your rice if you don’t vote for Vijay': How TVK reeled in a tsunami of votes in TN
content: Actor Vijay's political campaign mirrored a film release, utilizing digital marketing strategies like trailers and guest appearances to engage voters. His network of fan clubs acted as "virtual warriors," leveraging holograms, AI, and hyper-local messaging to attract first-time voters and their families, proving highly effective.

title: Bengaluru college professor’s charred body in car, her lover’s in pieces on railway tracks
content: A tragic end befell a divorced man and a college professor in Bengaluru. The woman's charred body was discovered in a car, while the man's dismembered remains were found on railway tracks. Police suspect a violent confrontation led to the woman's death, followed by the man's apparent suicide.

title: 'Jai Shree Ram … ': A note, a prayer, and Raghu Sharma's 15-year wait
content: Mumbai Indians' Raghu Sharma celebrated his maiden IPL wicket with an emotional message, marking the end of a grueling 15-year journey.  The leg-spinner, who overcame injuries and career setbacks, dedicated his breakthrough moment to his faith and the franchise that gave him a second chance. This personal triumph overshadowed even a vintage Rohit Sharma performance.

title: TVK storms Tamil Nadu but falls short of majority: Who will Vijay ally with?
content: Mega movie star "Thalapathy" Vijay's party, Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), has emerged as a significant force in Tamil Nadu politics, disrupting the DMK and AIADMK's dominance. While TVK secured triple-digit leads in early trends, it fell short of a majority, prompting debate on structural shifts versus celebrity appeal and raising questions about Vijay's solo political path.

title: Assembly polls decoded: Beyond headlines and numbers - 10 trends behind the verdict
content: Recent state election results reveal a resurgence of anti-incumbency, Hindu vote consolidation, and the enduring appeal of Narendra Modi. Factors like women's safety concerns, the rise of cash transfer schemes, and Gen Z's political awakening also played significant roles. The article delves into these ten decisive elements shaping India's electoral landscape.

title: ‘Virat Kohli, call me!’: IShowSpeed’s wild cricket clip goes viral - Watch
content: Internet personality IShowSpeed has gone viral again, this time with a cricket bat, calling out to Virat Kohli in a high-energy video. The clip shows Speed hitting the stumps and expressing his desire for Kohli and the ICC to notice him. This latest stunt further highlights his growing fascination with cricket and the Indian superstar.

title: Assembly elections result: BJP is Bengal Janata’s Party, Vijay Divas in TN, Keralove for Congress
content: BJP finally managed to win the state that had virtually become its final frontier over the past 10 years . BJP superstar Vijay made a stunning political debut and looks set to be the next chief minister of Tamil Nadu . Congress got the better of CPM in Kerala, clearing the way for a Left-mukt Bharat .

title: West Bengal verdict: Why Mamata tasted defeat when she put fish on plate?
content: Mamata Banerjee framed the contest as a battle for Bengali identity against a hostile central govt . she drew attention to migrant workers being targeted in other states . BJP's alleged dictates on non-veg food and the Centre’s apathy in releasing funds .
